title: Sarga 35
summary: Rama's divine birth, childhood, protection of Sage Vishwamitra's rituals from demons, breaking of Shiva's bow, and marriage to Sita. Addresses youth, childhood, education, overcoming early obstacles, and marriage.

sections.summary: This passage begins with “Then Rama, with folded hands and full of humility, asked the sage (Agastya) in the hermitage of the southern direction, speaking these meaningful words.” and develops toward “I believe Hanuman did not know his own strength, because he saw Sugriva, who was dear to him as life, suffering.”

This passage begins with “O revered one, worshipped by gods, please tell me all about the great-minded Hanuman in detail and truly.” and develops toward “The child, facing the morning sun, soared through the middle of the sky like an embodied morning sun, desirous of seizing the sun itself.”

This passage begins with “Indeed, a very great wonder arose among the gods, demons, and yakshas as this child Hanumān soared through the sky.” and develops toward “Having heard Rahu's words, Vasava (Indra), filled with agitation, sprang up, abandoning his seat and still wearing his golden garland.”

This passage begins with “Then (Indra's elephant) appeared, resembling the peak of Kailasa, four-tusked, with ichor flowing, adorned with ornaments, tall, and making a loud sound with its golden bells.” and develops toward “When the child, distressed by the thunderbolt's strike, fell, the wind god Vayu became angry at Indra, foreseeing harm to all creatures.”

This passage begins with “The lord Vayu, having withdrawn his circulation from among all creatures, took his child and entered a cave.” and develops toward “The bodiless Vayu moves within and protects all bodies, for indeed, a body without Vayu becomes equal to a mere log of wood.”

This passage begins with “Vayu is the life-breath, Vayu is happiness, and Vayu is this entire world, for the world finds no happiness when completely abandoned by Vayu.” and develops toward “Then, the four-faced Brahma, seeing the son, who shone with the radiance of the sun, fire, and gold, placed on Vayu's lap, then showed compassion…”

verses.sanskrit: अपृच्छत तदा रामो दक्षिणाशाश्रमं मुनिम् । प्राञ्जलिर्विनयोपेत इदमाह वचो ऽर्थवत् ।। 7.35.1 ।।
verses.translation: Then Rama, with folded hands and full of humility, asked the sage (Agastya) in the hermitage of the southern direction, speaking these meaningful words.

verses.sanskrit: अतुलं बलमेतद्वै वालिनो रावणस्य च । न त्वेताभ्यां हनुमता समं त्विति मतिर्मम ।। 7.35.2 ।।
verses.translation: This strength of Vali and Ravana is indeed incomparable, but in my opinion, it is not equal to Hanuman's.

verses.sanskrit: शौर्यं दाक्ष्यं बलं धैर्यं प्राज्ञता नयसाधनम् । विक्रमश्च प्रभावश्च हनूमति कृतालयाः ।। 7.35.3 ।।
verses.translation: Valor, skill, strength, courage, wisdom, diplomatic ability, prowess, and influence—all these qualities reside in Hanuman.

verses.sanskrit: दृष्ट्वैव सागरं वीक्ष्य सीदन्तीं कपिवाहिनीम् । समाश्वास्य महाबाहुर्योजनानां शतं प्लुतः ।। 7.35.4 ।।
verses.translation: Having observed the ocean and seeing the monkey army despairing, the mighty-armed Hanuman comforted them and then leaped a hundred yojanas across the sea.

verses.sanskrit: धर्षयित्वा पुरीं लङ्कां रावणान्तःपुरं तदा । दृष्ट्वा सम्भाषिता चापि सीता ह्याश्वासिता तथा ।। 7.35.5 ।।
verses.translation: Then, having harassed the city of Lanka and Ravana's inner palace, he indeed saw Sita, conversed with her, and thus comforted her.

verses.sanskrit: सेनाग्रगा मन्त्रिसुताः किङ्करा रावणात्मजः । एते हनुमता तत्र ह्येकेन वितिपातिताः ।। 7.35.6 ।।
verses.translation: The leaders of the army, the sons of ministers, the attendants, and Ravana's own son—all these were indeed struck down there by Hanuman alone.

verses.sanskrit: भूयो बन्धाद्विमुक्तेन भाषयित्वा दशाननम् । लङ्का भस्मीकृता येन पावकेनेव मेदिनी ।। 7.35.7 ।।
verses.translation: By him, who, having been released from bondage, spoke to Daśānana, Lanka was reduced to ashes, as if the earth itself were consumed by fire.

verses.sanskrit: न कालस्य न शक्रस्य न विष्णोर्वित्तपस्य च । कर्माणि तानि श्रूयन्ते यानि युद्धे हनूमतः ।। 7.35.8 ।।
verses.translation: Such deeds as those of Hanumān in battle are not heard of, even from Time (Yama), Indra, Vishnu, or Kubera.

verses.sanskrit: एतस्य बाहुवीर्येण लङ्का सीता च लक्ष्मणः । प्राप्ता मया जयश्चैव राज्यं मित्राणि बान्धवाः ।। 7.35.9 ।।
verses.translation: By the valor of Hanumān, I regained Lanka, Sītā, Lakshmaṇa, victory, my kingdom, friends, and relatives.

verses.sanskrit: हनूमान्यदि मे नः स्याद्वानराधिपतेः सखा । प्रवृत्तिमपि को वेत्तुं जानक्याः शक्तिमान्भवेत् ।। 7.35.10 ।।
verses.translation: If Hanumān, the friend of the lord of monkeys, had not been with me, who would have been capable of even finding news of Janakī?

verses.sanskrit: किमर्थं वाल्यनेनैव सुग्रीवप्रियकाम्यया । तदा वैरे समुत्पन्ने न दग्धो वीरुधो यथा ।। 7.35.11 ।।
verses.translation: Why then, when enmity had arisen, was Vāli not burnt by Hanumān, out of a desire to please Sugrīva, just like creepers are burnt?

verses.sanskrit: नहि वेदितवान्मन्ये हनूमानात्मनो बलम् । यद्दृष्टवाञ्जीवितेष्टं क्लिश्यन्तं वानराधिपम् ।। 7.35.12 ।।
verses.translation: I believe Hanuman did not know his own strength, because he saw Sugriva, who was dear to him as life, suffering.

verses.sanskrit: एतन्मे भगवन्सर्वं हनूमति महामतौ । विस्तरेण यथातत्त्वं कथयामरपूजित ।। 7.35.13 ।।
verses.translation: O revered one, worshipped by gods, please tell me all about the great-minded Hanuman in detail and truly.

verses.sanskrit: राघवस्य वचः श्रुत्वा हेतुयुक्तमृषिस्तदा । हनूमतः समक्षं तमिदं वचनमब्रवीत् ।। 7.35.14 ।।
verses.translation: Having heard Rama's reasonable words, the sage then spoke these words to him in Hanuman's presence.

verses.sanskrit: सत्यमेतद्रघुश्रेष्ठ यद्ब्रवीषि हनूमतः । न बले विद्यते तुल्यो न गतौ न मतौ परः ।। 7.35.15 ।।
verses.translation: O best of Raghus, what you say about Hanuman is true: there is no one equal to him in strength, speed, or intellect.

verses.sanskrit: अमोघशापैः शप्तस्तु दत्तो ऽस्य मुनिभिः पुरा । न वेत्ता हि बलं येन बली सन्निरिमर्दनः ।। 7.35.16 ।।
verses.translation: Indeed, he was formerly cursed by sages with unfailing curses, by which, though he is strong and a destroyer of foes, he does not know his own strength.

verses.sanskrit: बाल्ये ऽप्येतेन यत्कर्म कृतं राम महाबल । तन्न वर्णयितुं शक्यमिति बालतया ऽ ऽस्यते ।। 7.35.17 ।।
verses.translation: O mighty Rama, it is impossible to fully describe the deeds performed by him (Hanuman) even in his childhood, as they are beyond words due to his tender age.

verses.sanskrit: यदि वा ऽस्ति त्वभिप्रायस्तच्छ्रोतुं तव राघव । समाधाय मतिं राम निशामय वदाम्यहम् ।। 7.35.18 ।।
verses.translation: O Raghava, if you indeed have a desire to hear those deeds, then O Rama, concentrate your mind and listen, for I shall narrate them.

verses.sanskrit: सूर्यदत्तवरस्वर्णः सुमेरुर्नाम पर्वतः । यत्र राज्यं प्रशास्त्यस्य केसरी नाम वै पिता ।। 7.35.19 ।।
verses.translation: There is a mountain named Sumeru, golden due to a boon granted by the Sun, where his (Hanuman's) father, named Kesari, indeed rules.

verses.sanskrit: तस्य भार्या बभूवैषा ह्यञ्जनेति परिश्रुता । जनयामास तस्यां वै वायुरात्मजमुत्तमम् ।। 7.35.20 ।।
verses.translation: His (Kesari's) well-known wife was Anjana, in whom Vayu, the Wind-god, indeed begot an excellent son.

verses.sanskrit: शालिशूकनिभाभासं प्रासूतामुं तदा ऽञ्जना । फलान्याहर्तुकामा वै निष्क्रान्ता गहनेचरा ।। 7.35.21 ।।
verses.translation: Anjana, who moved in the forest, then gave birth to him (Hanuman), who had a luster like a ripe paddy grain, and she had gone out desiring to gather fruits.

verses.sanskrit: एष मातुर्वियोगाच्च क्षुधया च भृशार्दितः । रुरोद शिशुरत्यर्थं शिशुः शरवणे यथा ।। 7.35.22 ।।
verses.translation: This child, greatly distressed by separation from his mother and by hunger, cried exceedingly, just like the child Kartikeya in the reed-forest.

verses.sanskrit: तदोद्यन्तं विवस्वन्तं जपापुष्पोत्करोपमम् । ददर्श फललोभाच्च ह्युत्पपात रविं प्रति ।। 7.35.23 ।।
verses.translation: Then, he saw the rising sun, which resembled a cluster of hibiscus flowers, and out of a desire for fruit, he indeed flew up towards the sun.

verses.sanskrit: बालार्काभिमुखो बालो बालार्क इव मूर्तिमान् । ग्रहीतुकामो बालार्कं प्लवते ऽम्बरमध्यगः ।। 7.35.24 ।।
verses.translation: The child, facing the morning sun, soared through the middle of the sky like an embodied morning sun, desirous of seizing the sun itself.

verses.sanskrit: एतस्मिन्प्लवमाने तु शिशुभावे हनूमति । देवदानवयक्षाणां विस्मयः सुमहानभूत् ।। 7.35.25 ।।
verses.translation: Indeed, a very great wonder arose among the gods, demons, and yakshas as this child Hanumān soared through the sky.

verses.sanskrit: नाप्येवं वेगवान्वायुर्गरुडो वा मनस्तथा । यथा ऽयं वायुपुत्रस्तु क्रमते ऽम्बरमुत्तमम् ।। 7.35.26 ।।
verses.translation: Neither the wind, nor Garuda, nor even the mind is as swift as this son of Vayu, who indeed traverses the high sky.

verses.sanskrit: यदि तावच्छिशोरस्य त्वीदृशो गतिविक्रमः । यौवनं बलमासाद्य कथं वेगो भविष्यति ।। 7.35.27 ।।
verses.translation: If this child, even in his infancy, possesses such speed and prowess, how much greater will his velocity become when he attains youth and full strength?

verses.sanskrit: तमनुप्लवते वायुः प्लवन्तं पुत्रमात्मनः । सूर्यदाहभयाद्रक्षंस्तुषारचयशीतलः ।। 7.35.28 ।।
verses.translation: Vayu, the wind god, followed his own son (Hanuman) as he flew, protecting him from the sun's scorching heat by making himself cool with a mass of frost.

verses.sanskrit: बहुयोजनसाहस्रं क्रमत्येष गतोम्बरम् । पितुर्बलाच्च बाल्याच्च भास्कराभ्याशमागतः ।। 7.35.29 ।।
verses.translation: This child, having traversed many thousands of yojanas into the sky, reached the vicinity of the sun, driven by his father's strength and his own childish innocence.

verses.sanskrit: शिशुरेष त्वदोषज्ञ इति मत्वा दिवाकरः । कार्यं चात्र समायत्तमित्येवं न ददाह सः ।। 7.35.30 ।।
verses.translation: Considering this child to be innocent and realizing that a significant purpose was dependent on him, the sun did not burn him.

verses.sanskrit: यमेव दिवसं ह्येष ग्रहीतुं भास्करं प्लुतः । तमेव दिवसं राहुर्जिघृक्षति दिवाकरम् ।। 7.35.31 ।।
verses.translation: On the very day that this child leaped to seize the sun, Rahu also desired to grasp the sun.

verses.sanskrit: अनेन स परामृष्टो राम सूर्यरथोपरि । अपक्रान्तस्ततस्त्रस्तो राहुश्चन्द्रार्कमर्दनः ।। 7.35.32 ।।
verses.translation: O Rama, when he (the original Rahu), the tormentor of the moon and sun, was touched by that other one upon the sun's chariot, he became frightened and departed from there.

verses.sanskrit: स इन्द्रभवनं गत्वा सरोषः सिंहिकासुतः । अब्रवीद्भ्रुकुटिं कृत्वा देवं देवगणैर्वृतम् ।। 7.35.33 ।।
verses.translation: The enraged son of Simhika (Rahu), having gone to Indra's abode, spoke to the god (Indra) who was surrounded by hosts of gods, making a frown.

verses.sanskrit: बुभुक्षापनयं दत्त्वा चन्द्रार्कौ मम वासव । किमिदं तत्त्वया दत्तमन्यस्य बलवृत्रहन् ।। 7.35.34 ।।
verses.translation: O Vasava, O slayer of Bala and Vritra, having given the moon and sun to me as a means to satisfy my hunger, why have you now given them to another?

verses.sanskrit: अद्याहं पर्वकाले तु जिघृक्षुः सूर्यमागतः । अथान्यो राहुरासाद्य जग्राह सहसा रविम् ।। 7.35.35 ।।
verses.translation: Today, I had indeed arrived at the time of conjunction intending to seize the sun, but then another Rahu suddenly approached and seized the sun.

verses.sanskrit: स राहोर्वचनं श्रुत्वा वासवः सम्भ्रमान्वितः । उत्पपातासनं हित्वा चोद्वहन्काञ्चनीं स्रजम् ।। 7.35.36 ।।
verses.translation: Having heard Rahu's words, Vasava (Indra), filled with agitation, sprang up, abandoning his seat and still wearing his golden garland.

verses.sanskrit: ततः कैलासकूटाभं चतुर्दन्तं मदस्रवम् । शृङ्गारधारिणं प्रांशुं स्वर्णघण्टाट्टहासिनम् ।। 7.35.37 ।।
verses.translation: Then (Indra's elephant) appeared, resembling the peak of Kailasa, four-tusked, with ichor flowing, adorned with ornaments, tall, and making a loud sound with its golden bells.

verses.sanskrit: इन्द्रः करीन्द्रमारुह्य राहुं कृत्वा पुरःसरम् । प्रायाद्यत्राभवत्सूर्यः सहानेन हनूमता ।। 7.35.38 ।।
verses.translation: Indra, having mounted his lordly elephant Airavata and making Rahu go before him, went to where the sun was, along with Hanuman.

verses.sanskrit: अथातिरभसेनागाद्राहुरुत्सृज्य वासवम् । अनेन च स वै दृष्टः प्रधावञ्छैलकूटवत् ।। 7.35.39 ।।
verses.translation: Then Rahu, leaving Indra, went with great speed, and he was indeed seen by Hanuman running swiftly like a mountain peak.

verses.sanskrit: ततः सूर्यं समुत्सृज्य राहुं फलमवेक्ष्य च । उत्पपात पुनर्व्योम ग्रहीतुं सिंहिकासुतम् ।। 7.35.40 ।।
verses.translation: Then, having completely left the sun and considering Rahu as a fruit, Hanuman again sprang into the sky to seize the son of Simhika.

verses.sanskrit: उत्सृज्यार्कमिमं राम प्रधावन्तं प्लवङ्गमम् । अवेक्ष्यैवं परावृत्त्य मुखशेषः पराङ्मुखः ।। 7.35.41 ।।
verses.translation: O Rama, having left the sun, Rahu, who has only a head remaining, turned away, averting his face after seeing this monkey (Hanuman) running swiftly.

verses.sanskrit: इन्द्रमाशंसमानस्तु त्रातारं सिंहिकासुतः । इन्द्र इन्द्रेति संत्रासान्मुहुर्महुरभाषत ।। 7.35.42 ।।
verses.translation: Simhika's son, Rahu, desiring Indra as his protector, cried out "O Indra! O Indra!" repeatedly out of great fear.

verses.sanskrit: राहोर्विक्रोशमानस्य प्रागेवालक्षितं स्वरम् । श्रुत्वेन्द्रोवाच मा भैषीरहमेनं निषूदये ।। 7.35.43 ।।
verses.translation: Having already perceived the voice of Rahu crying out, Indra said, "Do not fear; I will destroy him."

verses.sanskrit: ऐरावतं ततो दृष्ट्वा महत्तदिदमित्यपि । फलं मत्वा हस्तिराजमभिदुद्राव मारुतिः ।। 7.35.44 ।।
verses.translation: Then, having seen Airavata, Maruta's son (Hanuman) thought, "This is a huge fruit," and rushed towards the king of elephants.

verses.sanskrit: तथास्य धावतो रूपमैरावतजिघृक्षया । मुहूर्तमभवद्घोरमिन्द्राग्न्योरिव भास्वरम् ।। 7.35.45 ।।
verses.translation: As he ran with the desire to seize Airavata, his form became terrible and shone brilliantly for a moment, like that of Indra and Agni.

verses.sanskrit: एवमाधावमानं तु नातिक्रुद्धः शचीपतिः । हस्तान्तादतिमुक्तेन कुलिशेनाभ्यताडयत् ।। 7.35.46 ।।
verses.translation: Indra, Shachi's husband, though not excessively angry, struck him as he rushed forward with a thunderbolt released from his hand.

verses.sanskrit: ततो गिरौ पपातैष इन्द्रवज्राभिताडितः । पतमानस्य चैतस्य वामो हनुरभज्यत ।। 7.35.47 ।।
verses.translation: Struck by Indra's thunderbolt, the child fell onto the mountain, and as he fell, his left jaw was broken.

verses.sanskrit: तस्मिंस्तु पतिते बाले वज्रताडनविह्वले । चुक्रोधेन्द्राय पवनः प्रजानामहिताय सः ।। 7.35.48 ।।
verses.translation: When the child, distressed by the thunderbolt's strike, fell, the wind god Vayu became angry at Indra, foreseeing harm to all creatures.

verses.sanskrit: प्रचारं स तु सङ्गृह्य प्रजास्वन्तर्गतः प्रभुः । गुहां प्रविष्टः स्वसुतं शिशुमादाय मारुतः ।। 7.35.49 ।।
verses.translation: The lord Vayu, having withdrawn his circulation from among all creatures, took his child and entered a cave.

verses.sanskrit: विण्मूत्राशयमावृत्य प्रजानां परमार्तिकृत् । रुरोध सर्वभूतानि यथा वर्षाणि वासवः ।। 7.35.50 ।।
verses.translation: Causing great distress to creatures by obstructing their excretory functions, he stopped all beings, just as Indra stops the rains.

verses.sanskrit: वायुप्रकोपाद्भूतानि निरुच्छ्वासानि सर्वतः । सन्धिभिर्भिद्यमानैश्च काष्ठभूतानि जज्ञिरे ।। 7.35.51 ।।
verses.translation: Due to the wrath of Vayu, creatures everywhere became breathless, and with their joints splitting, they became like lifeless wood.

verses.sanskrit: निःस्वाध्यायवषट्कारं निष्क्रियं धर्मवर्जितम् । वायुप्रकोपात्ऺत्रैलोक्यं निरयस्थमिवाभवत् ।। 7.35.52 ।।
verses.translation: Due to the wrath of Vayu, the three worlds became as if situated in hell, devoid of Vedic study, oblations, religious activities, and righteousness.

verses.sanskrit: ततः प्रजाः सगन्धर्वाः सदेवासुरमानुषाः । प्रजापतिं समाधावन्दुःखिताश्च सुखेच्छया ।। 7.35.53 ।।
verses.translation: Then, all beings including Gandharvas, Devas, Asuras, and humans, distressed and desiring happiness, rushed to Prajapati Brahma.

verses.sanskrit: ऊचुः प्राञ्जलयो देवा महोदरनिभोदराः । त्वया नु भगवन्सृष्टाः प्रजानाथ चतुर्विधाः ।। 7.35.54 ।।
verses.translation: With folded hands, the gods, whose bellies appeared distended from suffering, addressed Prajapati, saying, "O Lord of creatures, indeed, the four kinds of beings were created by you."

verses.sanskrit: त्वया दत्तो ऽयमस्माकमायुषः पवनः पतिः । सो ऽस्मान्प्राणेश्वरो भूत्वा कस्मादेषो ऽद्य सत्तम ।। 7.35.55 ।।
verses.translation: "This Vayu, the lord of our life, was given to us by you; why then, O best among the good, has he, having become the lord of our very breath, acted thus today?"

verses.sanskrit: रुरोध दुःखं जनयन्नन्तःपुर इव स्त्रियः । तस्मात्त्वां शरणं प्राप्ता वायुनोहता वयम् ।। 7.35.56 ।।
verses.translation: "He has obstructed us, causing sorrow, just as women are confined in a harem; therefore, we, afflicted by Vayu, have sought refuge in you."

verses.sanskrit: एतत्प्रजानां श्रुत्वा तु प्रजानाथः प्रजापतिः । कारणादिति चोक्त्वासौ प्रजाः पुनरभाषत ।। 7.35.57 ।।
verses.translation: Having heard this from the people, Prajapati, the lord of creation, then spoke to them again, saying "due to the reason."

verses.sanskrit: यस्मिंश्च कारणे वायुश्चुक्रोध च रुरोध च । प्रजाः शृणुध्वं तत्सर्वं श्रोतव्यं चात्मनः क्षमम् ।। 7.35.58 ।।
verses.translation: Prajapati then instructed the people to listen to the entire beneficial reason for which Vayu became angry and caused obstruction.

verses.sanskrit: पुत्रस्तस्यामरेशेन इन्द्रेणाद्य निपातितः । राहोर्वचनमास्थाय ततः स कुपितो ऽनिलः ।। 7.35.59 ।।
verses.translation: Vayu's son was struck down by Indra, the lord of immortals, today, acting upon the advice of Rahu, which is why Vayu became enraged.

verses.sanskrit: अशरीरः शरीरेषु वायुश्चरति पालयन् । शरीरं हि विना वायुं समतां याति दारुभिः ।। 7.35.60 ।।
verses.translation: The bodiless Vayu moves within and protects all bodies, for indeed, a body without Vayu becomes equal to a mere log of wood.

verses.sanskrit: वायुः प्राणः सुखं वायुर्वायुः सर्वमिदं जगत् । वायुना सम्परित्यक्तं न सुखं विन्दते जगत् ।। 7.35.61 ।।
verses.translation: Vayu is the life-breath, Vayu is happiness, and Vayu is this entire world, for the world finds no happiness when completely abandoned by Vayu.

verses.sanskrit: अद्यैव च परित्यक्तं वायुना जगदायुषा । अद्यैव ते निरुच्छ्वासाः काष्ठकुड्योपमाः स्थिताः ।। 7.35.62 ।।
verses.translation: Just today, the life-giver of the world, Vayu, has abandoned them, and just today, they remain breathless, like inanimate wooden walls.

verses.sanskrit: तद्यामस्तत्र यत्रास्ते मारुतो रुक्प्रदो हि नः । मा विनाशं गमिष्याम अप्रसाद्यादितेः सुताः ।। 7.35.63 ।।
verses.translation: Therefore, let us go to where Vayu, who is indeed causing us pain, resides, lest we, the sons of Aditi, meet destruction without appeasing him.

verses.sanskrit: ततः प्रजाभिः सहितः प्रजापतिः सदेवगन्धर्वभुजङ्गगुह्यकैः । जगाम तत्रास्यति यत्र मारुतः सुतं सुरेन्द्राभिहतं प्रगृह्य सः ।। 7.35.64 ।।
verses.translation: Then, Prajapati, accompanied by all beings, including gods, gandharvas, serpents, and guhyakas, went to where Vayu sat, holding his son who had been struck by the lord of gods.

verses.sanskrit: ततो ऽर्कवैश्वानरकाञ्चनप्रभं सुतं तदोत्सङ्गगतं सदागतेः । चतुर्मुखो वीक्ष्य कृपामथाकरोत्सदेवगन्धर्वर्षियक्षराक्षसैः ।। 7.35.65 ।।
verses.translation: Then, the four-faced Brahma, seeing the son, who shone with the radiance of the sun, fire, and gold, placed on Vayu's lap, then showed compassion along with the gods, gandharvas, rishis, yakshas, and rakshasas. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीये आदिकाव्ये श्रीमदुत्तरकाण्डे पंचत्रिंशः सर्गः ।। 35 ।। Thus ends the thirty-fifth sarga of Uttara Kanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
